name: Foundation Software Construction Accounting API
what_it_does: Foundation Software construction accounting REST API for job costing, construction payroll, accounts payable, and financial management for contractors and construction companies. Enables AI agents to manage job cost tracking and budget variance analysis for construction financial management, handle certified payroll and Davis-Bacon prevailing wage for construction compliance automation, access accounts payable and subcontractor payment for construction cash flow management, retrieve WIP (Work in Progress) schedule and overbilling/underbilling analysis for construction project accounting, manage equipment cost tracking and depreciation for construction asset management, handle AIA billing and Schedule of Values for general contractor billing automation, access union payroll and fringe benefit calculation for union construction compliance, retrieve job profitability and company financial reporting for construction business intelligence, manage subcontractor lien waiver and compliance for construction risk management, and integrate Foundation with Procore, construction project management, and HR platforms.
use_cases: Tracking job costs from construction accounting agents
Processing certified payroll from compliance agents
Managing subcontractor payments from AP agents
Generating AIA billing from project billing agents
not_for: Technology or service firms without construction payroll
best_when: A general contractor, specialty contractor, or construction company using Foundation Software wanting AI agents to automate job costing, certified payroll, AP processing, WIP schedule, AIA billing, and construction financial reporting.
avoid_when: DAVIS-BACON PREVAILING WAGE COMPLIANCE: Automated certified payroll for public works must apply correct Davis-Bacon or state prevailing wage rates by classification and jurisdiction; automated payroll with incorrect wage rates creates DOL back-pay liability and contract debarment risk. Union fringe benefit accuracy — automated union payroll must correctly calculate and remit union health, pension, and annuity fringe benefits per applicable CBA; automated fringe miscalculation creates union trust fund audit and ERISA liability. AIA billing accuracy — automated AIA G702/G703 Application for Payment must accurately represent completed work percentages; automated overbilling creates contract dispute and construction lien exposure. Lien waiver management — automated subcontractor payment must include conditional lien waiver exchange; automated payment without lien waiver creates mechanics lien on project property.

auth.notes: Foundation Software uses API key authentication. REST API with JSON. Strongsville, Ohio HQ. Founded 1985. Private (private equity). Construction accounting software. 40,000+ contractor users. Heavy in certified payroll and union payroll. Procore certified integration. Competes with Sage 300 CRE, Viewpoint Vista, and CMiC for construction accounting.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — per-user or per-company annual subscription
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual subscription; accounting and payroll modules
pricing.notes: Strongsville, Ohio. Private equity-backed. Founded 1985. 40,000+ contractor users. Certified payroll and union specialty.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key; documented in integration documentation

known_agent_gotchas: DAVIS-BACON WAGE RATE ACCURACY: Automated certified payroll must apply current DOL wage determination rates by trade classification and county; incorrect rates create DOL back-pay liability and federal contract debarment; update wage rates from DOL WD.gov before each automated payroll run

Union fringe benefit remittance — automated union payroll fringe calculation must match collective bargaining agreement (CBA) fringe schedules exactly; automated fringe underpayment creates union trust fund delinquency and ERISA violation
No webhooks — Foundation Software lacks webhook support; AP approval completion and payroll events require polling; implement scheduled polling for event-driven construction accounting workflows

AIA percentage complete accuracy — automated AIA G702/G703 completion percentage must reflect actual work-in-place verified by project manager; automated overbilling creates owner disputes and potential contractor fraud liability

Lien waiver prerequisite for payment — automated subcontractor payment workflow must require conditional lien waiver before payment release; automated payment without lien waiver creates property lien exposure

Certified payroll state compliance — certified payroll requirements vary by state (Federal Davis-Bacon, state prevailing wage, and Little Davis-Bacon acts); automated certified payroll must apply correct state-specific requirements per project location
agent_readiness.security_notes: Construction accounting. SOC2. API key. US. Job cost and payroll data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Construction accounting. SOC2. API key. US. Job cost and construction payroll data.
